modifications: lto added with 'thin' setting for link-time optimization
file_insights.scope: root workspace release profile affecting all crates
file_insights.impact: binary size increase, improved runtime performance, longer compile times

patterns_discovered.description: Shift from space-constrained optimization ('s') to speed-optimized compilation (level 3)
patterns_discovered.context: Part of performance optimization initiative in bop project

patterns_discovered.description: Introduction of thin link-time optimization balances compile time vs runtime performance
patterns_discovered.context: Follows common Rust optimization patterns for production releases

patterns_discovered.description: Root Cargo.toml settings propagate to entire Rust workspace
patterns_discovered.context: Multi-crate workspace with standard profile inheritance

gotchas_discovered.issue: opt-level = 3 produces larger binaries than opt-level = 's'
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Acceptable if runtime performance is prioritized over deployment size

gotchas_discovered.issue: Thin LTO increases compilation duration, 'thin' is compromise between 'true' (slower) and 'fat' (not applicable)
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Use 'thin' as sweet spot; monitor CI/CD build times

gotchas_discovered.issue: Settings apply to all crates including debug/auxiliary binaries
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider profile overrides for specific crates if needed

approach_outcome.testing_implied: Build profile changes typically tested via cargo build/check in CI

recommendations.rationale: Verify that opt-level 3 + thin LTO provides meaningful performance gains in target use cases (VM dispatch, card processing)
Thin LTO increases compilation overhead; document impact on automation pipeline cycles
If auxiliary binaries (dub, test harnesses) don't need speed optimization, can reduce compile time with targeted profiles
Justify shift from size to speed optimization for future maintainers understanding bop design decisions
